case_title: BRIAN GRASSER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100042232) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRASSER, BRIAN AS TO DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOES 1 TO 20, INCLUSIVE
OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCOS MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100046402) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRASSER, BRIAN
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S COUNT ONE-NEGLIGENCE FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO PURSUE COUNT THREE FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY PER GOV. CODE, 835. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ANNE CHRISTINE MASSULLO; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: REBECCAR TOVAR, CSR # 14200 =302/ACM

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Thomas S. Lakritz, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. No.;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff's Count One-negligence fails as a matter of law. Plaintiff's remedy is to pursue Count Three for dangerous condition of public property per Gov. Code, 835. Order signed. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Rebeccar Tovar, CSR # 14200 =302/acm

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Thomas S. Lakritz, Deputy City Attorney, Tel. No.;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT - Defendant CCSF's demurrer to the second cause of action for "general negligence" is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND because it fails to state a cause of action. (Code Civ. Proc., 430.10(e).) Plaintiff alleges a dangerous condition of public property and his exclusive remedy is to pursue a claim under Gov. Code, 835. (See Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 829.) In Longfellow v. County of San Luis Obispo (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 379, 383, the court stated:
With respect to the plaintiffs' cause of action pursuant to section 815.2 of the Government Code, the law was settled by Van Kempen v. Hayward Area Park etc. District (1972) 23 Cal.App.3d 822 [100 Cal.Rptr. 498], that public entity liability for property defects is not governed by the general rule of vicarious liability provided in section 815.2, but rather by the provisions in sections 830 to 835.4 of the Government Code. A public employee is not liable for injuries caused by a condition of public property where such condition exists because of any act or omission of such employee within the scope of his employment. (Gov. Code, 840.) This is specifically what the plaintiffs allege in this cause of action. That is, that a dangerous condition of public property existed which should have been repaired by an employee of the County working within the scope of his employment and that, therefore, the County may be vicariously liable for the employee's failure to act. However, since the employee is immune, the public entity cannot be held liable for the acts of the employee and plaintiffs have no such cause of action.
Paterno v. State of California (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 68 does not mandate a different result. Paterno criticized Longfellow only to the extent Longfellow barred a nuisance claim. The court further notes that if an act or omission of a CCSF employee caused the defective gate, the CCSF may be liable under the dangerous condition of public property cause of action. (See Gov. Code, 835(a) & (b).) The order is submitted for review and signature. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Rebeccar Tovar, CSR # 14200 =302/acm
